title: Funastrum elegans
taxonomy: Funastrum elegans is a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae. It is native to the Americas and is commonly known by several names, including coral milkweed and coral root. This plant is part of the Apocynaceae family, which also includes other milkweed species. Funastrum elegans is often found in dry, rocky areas and can be recognized by its clusters of small, white flowers. The plant is considered to be a perennial, meaning it regrows new leaves and stems each year.

intro: Funastrum elegans, also known as Devil's Claw vine or Cat's Claw vine, is a perennial climber native to the deserts of North and South America, characterized by its hook-like tendrils and distinctive, heart-shaped leaves. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include reducing inflammation and pain, as well as its potential to alleviate symptoms of anxiety and insomnia. The bioactive constituents of Funastrum elegans include flavonoids, alkaloids, and glycosides, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects.
